title: Tetraena clavata
taxonomy: Tetraena clavata is classified under the kingdom Plantae, with more specific classification as follows: it belongs to the family Zygophyllaceae, order Zygophyllales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta. This plant is native to Africa. Within the Zygophyllaceae family, Tetraena clavata is closely related to other desert-adapted species. The classification of Tetraena clavata reflects its unique evolutionary history and adaptations to its arid environment. Further research is needed to fully understand the taxonomy and relationships within this family.

properties: Tetraena clavata, also known as the climbing milk bush, is traditionally used in folk medicine for various health benefits.
